title: The Dying Need But Little, Dear,
content:
The dying need but little, dear, --
    A glass of water's all,
    A flower's unobtrusive face
    To punctuate the wall,

    A fan, perhaps, a friend's regret,
    And certainly that one
    No color in the rainbow
    Perceives when you are gone.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Dying Need But Little, Dear,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
